subject: PhD Topology
instructions: Work the following problems and show all work. Support all statements to the best of your ability. Answer Problems 6–15 with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

problems.text: Is the quotient space \(X=\mathbb{R}/\mathbb{Z}\) obtained from the reals \(\mathbb{R}\) by collapsing the integers to a point metrizable? Compute homology groups of \(X\).
Show that any continuous map \(f:\mathbb{R}P^3\to T^3\) is null-homotopic.
Let \(A\subset\mathbb{R}^3\) be the union of a countable family of lines in \(\mathbb{R}^3\). Prove that \(\mathbb{R}^3\setminus A\) is connected.
Does there exist a closed orientable \(n\)-manifold \(M\) with

Let \(p:X\to M_2\) be a covering map of an orientable surface of genus 2 that corresponds to the subgroup \(G\subset\pi_1(M_2)\) generated by \(\{a_1,b_1\}\) in
\[
\pi_1(M_2)=\langle a_1,a_2,b_1,b_2\mid [a_1,b_1][a_2,b_2]=1\rangle.
\]
Compute homology groups of \(X\).
Compute the Euler characteristic of the manifold \(M=S^2\times S^4\times\mathbb{C}P^{51}\times\mathbb{R}P^{99}\).
State the Lefschetz Fixed Point Theorem. Derive the Brouwer Fixed Point Theorem from the Lefschetz Fixed Point Theorem.
Give definitions of retraction, deformation retraction, retract, and deformation retract. Give an example of a retract \(A\) of a topological space which is not a deformation retract.
Suppose that \(A\) is a retract of \(X\). Is it true that \(H_i(A)\) is a direct summand of \(H_i(X)\)? What about \(H^i(A)\) and \(H^i(X)\)?
Give definition of the mapping cylinder. Is the Möbius band a mapping cylinder?
Give definition of the mapping cone. Is there a map \(f:X\to Y\) with the mapping cone \(C_f\) homeomorphic to \(\mathbb{R}P^3\)?
Which of the following spaces are topological groups?
Is \(\mathbb{R}^{\omega}\) connected in the uniform topology?
